title: EFT: Conflict Split
description: Three practical clinician-facing slides extracted from the EFT interventions practice deck.

sources.label: Vrana and Greenberg, Overview of Emotion-Focused Therapy

slides.title: Marker: critic versus experiencing self
slides.body: Use when one self-position attacks, coerces, or shames another.
bullets: Identify the exact critic message and its emotional tone.
Put the critic in one chair and the impacted self in the other.
Aim for softening, protection, and integration, not winning.

slides.title: Two-chair dialogue
slides.body: The therapist guides contact between positions while protecting both differentiation and emotional depth.
steps: Have the critic speak directly in second person.
Move chairs and deepen the receiving impact: shame, hurt, fear, anger.
Return to critic and ask what it is trying to prevent or secure.
Heighten the experiencer's need for respect, support, or permission.
Look for critic softening into care, standards, grief, or fear.

slides.title: When the dialogue stalls
rows: Ask what would happen if it stopped.
Protect and validate impact before response.
Return to voice, body, and direct address.
